file.content:
---
entrypoints:
  web:
    address: ":80"
    forwardedHeaders:
      insecure: true
  websecure:
    address: ":443"
    forwardedHeaders:
      insecure: true
  proxytcp:
    address: ":1080"

api:
  dashboard: true
  debug: true
  insecure: false

# log:
#   level: DEBUG

serversTransport:
  insecureSkipVerify: true

providers:
  docker:
    network: "proxy_network"
    exposedByDefault: false
    swarmMode: false
    watch: true
    constraints: "LabelRegex(`Project`, `eliberty\\..*`)"

  file:
    directory: /etc/traefik/dynamic/
    watch: true

input.query: traefik 3.x migration breaking changes configuration docker provider constraints swarmMode

content.text:
### Enable Docker Swarm Provider

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/routing-configuration/other-providers/swarm

Configures the Traefik Docker Swarm provider to listen on the specified endpoint. This can be defined via YAML, TOML, or CLI arguments.

```yaml
providers:
  swarm:
    endpoint: "tcp://127.0.0.1:2377"
```

```toml
[providers.swarm]
  endpoint = "tcp://127.0.0.1:2377"
```

```bash
--providers.swarm.endpoint=tcp://127.0.0.1:2377
```

--------------------------------

### Traefik v2 Docker Swarm Mode Configuration

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/migrate/v2-to-v3-details

Example of how to enable Swarm mode with the Docker provider in Traefik v2. This configuration is deprecated in v3.

```yaml
providers:
  docker:
    swarmMode: true
```

```toml
[providers.docker]
    swarmMode=true
```

```cli
--providers.docker.swarmMode=true
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Traefik for Multi-Layer Routing in Docker Swarm

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/expose/swarm/advanced

This configuration enables the File provider for Traefik in Docker Swarm mode, allowing dynamic configuration loading from a specified directory. It sets up essential entrypoints and integrates with Docker Swarm for service discovery.

```yaml
services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.4
    command:
      - "--api.dashboard=true"
      - "--providers.docker.swarmMode=true"
      - "--providers.docker.exposedbydefault=false"
      - "--providers.docker.network=traefik_proxy"
      - "--providers.file.directory=/etc/traefik/dynamic"  # Enable File provider
      - "--entrypoints.web.address=:80"
      - "--entrypoints.websecure.address=:443"
      - "--entrypoints.websecure.http.tls=true"
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
      - "8080:8080"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
    configs:
      - source: mlr-config
        target: /etc/traefik/dynamic/mlr.yml
    networks:
      - traefik_proxy
    deploy:
      placement:
        constraints:
          - node.role == manager

configs:
  mlr-config:
    file: ./dynamic/mlr.yml

networks:
  traefik_proxy:
    external: true

```

### Configuration Details for Migrating from Traefik v2 to v3 > Install Configuration Changes > SwarmMode

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/migrate/v2-to-v3-details

In Traefik v3, the Docker provider has been split into two distinct providers: the Docker provider (without Swarm support) and the Swarm provider (for Swarm support only). Consequently, the v2 approach of using the swarmMode flag within the Docker provider is no longer supported and will prevent Traefik from starting. To resolve this, users must migrate their configuration to use the dedicated Swarm provider instead of the Docker provider for Swarm-related tasks.

```yaml

```

--------------------------------

### Traefik v2 Docker Swarm Mode Configuration

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/migrate/v2-to-v3-details

Example of how to enable Swarm mode with the Docker provider in Traefik v2. This configuration is deprecated in v3.

```yaml
providers:
  docker:
    swarmMode: true
```

```toml
[providers.docker]
    swarmMode=true
```

```cli
--providers.docker.swarmMode=true
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Traefik for Multi-Layer Routing in Docker Swarm

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/expose/swarm/advanced

This configuration enables the File provider for Traefik in Docker Swarm mode, allowing dynamic configuration loading from a specified directory. It sets up essential entrypoints and integrates with Docker Swarm for service discovery.

```yaml
services:
  traefik:
    image: traefik:v3.4
    command:
      - "--api.dashboard=true"
      - "--providers.docker.swarmMode=true"
      - "--providers.docker.exposedbydefault=false"
      - "--providers.docker.network=traefik_proxy"
      - "--providers.file.directory=/etc/traefik/dynamic"  # Enable File provider
      - "--entrypoints.web.address=:80"
      - "--entrypoints.websecure.address=:443"
      - "--entrypoints.websecure.http.tls=true"
    ports:
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
      - "8080:8080"
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro

      - source: mlr-config
        target: /etc/traefik/dynamic/mlr.yml

      - traefik_proxy

      placement:
        constraints:
          - node.role == manager

configs:
  mlr-config:
    file: ./dynamic/mlr.yml

networks:
  traefik_proxy:
    external: true

```

### Configuration Details for Migrating from Traefik v2 to v3 > Install Configuration Changes > SwarmMode

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/migrate/v2-to-v3-details

In Traefik v3, the Docker provider has been split into two distinct providers: the Docker provider (without Swarm support) and the Swarm provider (for Swarm support only). Consequently, the v2 approach of using the swarmMode flag within the Docker provider is no longer supported and will prevent Traefik from starting. To resolve this, users must migrate their configuration to use the dedicated Swarm provider instead of the Docker provider for Swarm-related tasks.

input.query: traefik v2 to v3 migration breaking changes entrypoints forwardedHeaders constraints LabelRegex

content.text:
### Enable Traefik v2 Compatibility Mode

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/migrate/v2-to-v3

This configuration snippet enables backward compatibility for Traefik v3, allowing it to use v2 syntax for default rule matchers. This is crucial for a gradual migration process.

```yaml
# install configuration
core:
  defaultRuleSyntax: v2
```

--------------------------------

### Migrate Path Placeholders to PathRegexp

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/migrate/v2-to-v3-details

Illustrates the migration from v2 path placeholders to v3 PathRegexp syntax for dynamic path segments.

```text
# v2 syntax (no longer works in v3):
match: Host(`example.com`) && Path(`/products/{id}`)

# v3 syntax using PathRegexp:
match: Host(`example.com`) && PathRegexp(`^/products/[^/]+$`)
```

--------------------------------

### Entrypoint Forwarded Headers Configuration

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik-hub/api-gateway/reference/install/ref-install-conf

Configure how Traefik handles forwarded headers for security and trust.

```APIDOC
## Entrypoint Forwarded Headers Configuration

### Description
Configure how Traefik handles forwarded headers to trust specific IPs or all headers.

### Method
Not Applicable (Configuration)

### Endpoint
Not Applicable (Configuration)

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
None

### Request Example
None

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
None

#### Response Example
None

## Configuration Options

- `entrypoints.<name>.forwardedheaders.insecure` (bool) - Trust all forwarded headers. Default: `false`
- `entrypoints.<name>.forwardedheaders.trustedips` (list of strings) - Trust only forwarded headers from selected IPs.
```

--------------------------------

### Traefik Docker Constraints - Label Matching

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/providers/docker

Defines Traefik's behavior for including or excluding Docker containers based on their labels. It supports exact label matching and regular expression matching for flexible filtering. The `traefik.*` namespace is reserved and cannot be used for custom constraints.

```text
# Includes only containers having a label with key `a.label.name` and value `foo`
constraints = "Label(`a.label.name`, `foo`)"
```

```text
# Excludes containers having any label with key `a.label.name` and value `foo`
constraints = "!Label(`a.label.name`, `value`)"
```

```text
# Includes only containers having a label with key `a.label.name` and a value matching the `a.+` regular expression.
constraints = "LabelRegex(`a.label.name`, `a.+`)"
```

### Migration Guide: From v2 to v3 > Streamlined Migration Process

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/migrate/v2-to-v3

Traefik v3 introduces a streamlined transition process designed to minimize breaking changes while maintaining backward compatibility with v2 syntax in routing configurations. This approach offers a gradual migration path, allowing users to progressively update their Kubernetes ingress resources, Docker labels, and other configurations to the new v3 format without requiring a complete rewrite or experiencing downtime.

This approach offers a gradual migration path, allowing users to progressively update their Kubernetes ingress resources, Docker labels, and other configurations to the new v3 format without requiring a complete rewrite or experiencing downtime.

content.thinking:
Based on the docs, here are the key changes needed for Traefik 3.x:

1. `swarmMode: false` in the Docker provider - this option no longer exists in v3. Since it's `false`, simply removing it is fine.
2. The `watch: true` option in the Docker provider - need to check if this is still valid.
3. The `constraints` syntax with `LabelRegex` - the docs show `LabelRegex` is still valid in v3 but the syntax uses two arguments: `LabelRegex(\`label.name\`, \`pattern\`)`. The current config has `LabelRegex(\`Project\`, \`eliberty\\..*\`)` which looks like it's using the correct format.


4. The constraint format is already correct for v3, and `Project` isn't in the reserved `traefik.*` namespace so that's fine. The main breaking change is removing `swarmMode: false`, while `watch: true` remains valid in both versions. I should also verify if there are any changes to the entrypoint `forwardedHeaders` configuration. The key breaking change is removing `swarmMode: false` from the docker provider since Traefik v3 no longer supports this flag at all. The `watch` property is still valid, and the rest of the configuration should work as-is, so I just need to make that one minimal change.

content.text: The only breaking change in this config for Traefik v3 is `swarmMode: false` — the `swarmMode` option was removed from the Docker provider entirely in v3 (even `false` will prevent startup). Everything else (`forwardedHeaders`, `constraints` with `LabelRegex`, `serversTransport`, etc.) is compatible.

content.thinking:
Let me analyze each file for Traefik v3 compatibility:

**0-rtr-redirect-https.yml:**
The rule uses v2 syntax with `hostregexp` and path placeholders like `{host:.+.(loc|localhost)}`.

In Traefik v3:
- `hostregexp` with curly-brace placeholders is replaced by `HostRegexp` with pure regex
- v2 syntax: `hostregexp(`{host:.+.(loc|localhost)}`)`
- v3 syntax: `HostRegexp(`.+\.(loc|localhost)`)`


- The `HostRegexp` matcher no longer accepts named capture groups, just the regex pattern directly
- For redirects, the old approach of using a dummy service is outdated; v3 supports `redirectScheme` middleware, though routers still technically need a service reference
- The cleanest approach in v3 is configuring HTTP→HTTPS redirect at the entrypoint level rather than through middleware

, though the router/middleware approach still works if I fix the `HostRegexp` syntax to remove named capture groups. The `redirectScheme` middleware and TLS configuration don't need changes for v3, so I mainly need to update the router configuration to use the correct regex syntax. I'm checking whether v3 supports the old syntax in compatibility mode, but since the user wants a proper v3 migration rather than backwards compatibility, I should focus on the actual breaking changes. Looking at the files, only `0-rtr-redirect-https.yml` needs updating—the `hostregexp` rule needs to drop the named capture group syntax—while `middlewares.yml` and `tls.yml` are compatible as-is.

input.query: v2 to v3 HostRegexp rule syntax migration named capture groups hostregexp

content.text:
### Configure Router Rule Syntax via File Provider

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/routing-configuration/http/routing/rules-and-priority

Examples of configuring rule syntax for routers using YAML, TOML, labels, and tags to support migration between v2 and v3 syntax.

```yaml
http:
  routers:
    Router-v3:
      rule: HostRegexp(`[a-z]+\.traefik\.com`)
      ruleSyntax: v3
    Router-v2:
      rule: HostRegexp(`{subdomain:[a-z]+}.traefik.com`)
      ruleSyntax: v2
```

```toml
[http.routers]
  [http.routers.Router-v3]
    rule = "HostRegexp(`[a-z]+\\.traefik\\.com`)"
    ruleSyntax = v3
  [http.routers.Router-v2]
    rule = "HostRegexp(`{subdomain:[a-z]+}.traefik.com`)"
    ruleSyntax = v2
```

```docker-compose
labels:
  - "traefik.http.routers.Router-v3.rule=HostRegexp(`[a-z]+\\.traefik\\.com`)"
  - "traefik.http.routers.Router-v3.ruleSyntax=v3"
  - "traefik.http.routers.Router-v2.rule=HostRegexp(`{subdomain:[a-z]+}.traefik.com`)"
  - "traefik.http.routers.Router-v2.ruleSyntax=v2"
```

```json
"Tags": [
  "traefik.http.routers.Router-v3.rule=HostRegexp(`[a-z]+\\.traefik\\.com`)",
  "traefik.http.routers.Router-v3.ruleSyntax=v3",
  "traefik.http.routers.Router-v2.rule=HostRegexp(`{subdomain:[a-z]+}.traefik.com`)",
  "traefik.http.routers.Router-v2.ruleSyntax=v2"
]
```

--------------------------------

### Migrate Path Placeholders to PathRegexp

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/migrate/v2-to-v3-details

Illustrates the migration from v2 path placeholders to v3 PathRegexp syntax for dynamic path segments.

```text
# v2 syntax (no longer works in v3):
match: Host(`example.com`) && Path(`/products/{id}`)

# v3 syntax using PathRegexp:
match: Host(`example.com`) && PathRegexp(`^/products/[^/]+$`)
```

--------------------------------

### Define Host Matching Rules

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik-hub/api-gateway/reference/routing/http/routers/ref-rules-prios

Examples of using Host and HostRegexp matchers to filter requests based on the domain name or patterns.

```text
Host(`example.com`)
HostRegexp(`^.+\.example\.com$`)
HostRegexp(`^example\.(com|org)$`)
HostRegexp(`(?i)^example\.(com|org)$`)
```

### Router Rule Matchers > New V3 Syntax Notable Changes

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/migrate/v2-to-v3-details

The v3 syntax includes several breaking changes: matchers like Headers and HeadersRegexp are renamed to Header and HeaderRegexp, and PathPrefix no longer supports regular expressions. Additionally, path parameter placeholders like {id} are no longer supported in Path or PathPrefix matchers; users must now use PathRegexp with standard Go regular expressions to handle dynamic path segments. Other changes include the removal of HostHeader in favor of Host and the introduction of QueryRegexp for more flexible query parameter matching.

--------------------------------

### Router Rule Matchers > Migrate Path Placeholders to PathRegexp

Source: https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/migrate/v2-to-v3-details

Migrating from v2 path placeholders to v3 requires replacing placeholder syntax with explicit regular expressions. For example, a route previously defined as /products/{id} must be updated to use PathRegexp with a pattern like ^/products/[^/]+$. This change ensures that dynamic segments are handled consistently using Go's standard regular expression engine, allowing for more precise control over matching criteria.

content.text:
Only **one file needs changes**: [0-rtr-redirect-https.yml](config/traefik/include.d/0-rtr-redirect-https.yml)

The `hostregexp` rule uses v2 named-capture syntax (`{host:.+}`) which is invalid in v3. It needs to be converted to a plain regexp.

The other two files are fine:
- [middlewares.yml](config/traefik/include.d/middlewares.yml) — `redirectScheme` is unchanged in v3
- [tls.yml](config/traefik/include.d/tls.yml) — TLS stores/options config is unchanged

content.content: The file /home/xav/Workspace/docker-shared-ressources/config/traefik/include.d/0-rtr-redirect-https.yml has been updated successfully.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
http:
  routers:
    http-catchall:
      rule: "hostregexp(`{host:.+.(loc|localhost)}`)"
      entryPoints:
        - web
        # Websecure logically not needed, but fix brainless redirect to http on redpill root -> lang
        - websecure
      middlewares:
        - redirect-to-https
      service: "httpsredirector"

  services:
    httpsredirector:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "http://127.0.0.1:80"

lines: # Websecure logically not needed, but fix brainless redirect to http on redpill root -> lang

content.text:
**Change:** `hostregexp(\`{host:.+.(loc|localhost)}\`)` → `HostRegexp(\`.+\\.(loc|localhost)\`)`

- Removed named capture group `{host:...}` — v3 takes the regexp directly
- Escaped the dot before `(loc|localhost)` as `\\.` (was a pre-existing bug matching any character, not just `.`)
- `hostregexp` → `HostRegexp` (v3 is case-sensitive, though Traefik was lenient before)
